[11060-1] Reducing substances [Presence] in Stool -- **Component:** Reducing substances **Property:** PrThr **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Stool **Scale:** Ord  **Short name:** Reducing Subs Stl Ql  **Definition:** This term represents typical reducing substance assays that detect reducing substances in stool without (or before) the addition of HCl. These assays do not detect sugars that are not reducing, such as sucrose. A different code [LOINC: 57796-5] represents the stool reducing substance assay with the addition of HCl, which hydrolyzes sugars that are not typically reducing and converts them into a reducing form.

[111478-4] Influenza virus A whole genome segment sequence [Identifier] in Specimen by Sequencing -- **Component:** Influenza virus A whole genome segment sequence **Property:** ID **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** XXX **Scale:** Nom **Method:** Sequencing  **Short name:** FLUAV whole genome ID Spec Seq  **Definition:** USDA NAHLN labs regulatory test. Influenza A whole-genome sequencing assay is a molecular diagnostic technique that uses next-generation sequencing (NGS) technologies to identify the full nucleotide sequence of all eight RNA segments of the Influenza A virus. This assay enables comprehensive genetic characterization, surveillance of viral evolution, detection of mutations, and lineage classification. The labs part of the NAHLN network report this assay as a GenBank number. These accession numbers are used for referencing, data sharing, and comparative analysis in global influenza surveillance and research.
[111479-2] Advanced Explanation of Benefits -- **Component:** Advanced explanation of benefits **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Aeob  **Definition:** An advanced explanation of benefits (AEOB) is a document that gives patients an early, detailed estimate of the costs for scheduled medical services. It includes information about which providers will treat the patient, whether those providers are in-network, estimated charges and out-of-pocket costs, progress toward meeting deductibles, and if any services require special approval. The AEOB also explains that the costs shown are just estimates based on current information and may change before the actual service.

[111480-0] Good Faith Estimate -- **Component:** Good faith estimate **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** {Setting} **Scale:** Doc **Method:** {Role}  **Short name:** Gfe  **Definition:** The Good Faith Estimate (GFE) is a formal notification provided to patients that details the reasonably expected charges, billing codes, and services for a scheduled or requested healthcare item or service. The GFE includes an itemized estimate based on information from the provider, aiming to enhance transparency and enable informed patient decisions regarding healthcare costs. The full requirements for GFE content are specified in 42 U.S. Code § 300gg-136 and cover the expected charges, billing and diagnostic codes, and other elements necessary for clear patient cost communication.

[111530-2] Housing Question Set Condensed [Housing Question Set Condensed] -- **Component:** Housing Question Set Condensed **Property:** - **Time aspect:** RptPeriod **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** Housing Question Set Condensed  **Definition:** The Housing Question Set Condensed Version was developed for public health case interviews and surveillance. The question set includes three components that are asked for any relevant period: 1) whether the respondent stayed in one place or several; 2) open-ended description of the places where the respondent stayed, which the interviewer then matched to a prespecified list; and 3) multiple-choice questions to clarify the housing type, such as whether the respondent's current arrangement was short- or long-term.

[111770-4] Total score [AIMS] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** AIMS  **Definition:** Scoring Procedure Complete the examination procedure before making ratings. For the movement ratings (the first three categories below), rate the highest severity observed. 0 = none, 1 = minimal (may be extreme normal), 2 = mild, 3 = moderate, and 4 = severe. According to the original AIMS instructions, one point is subtracted if movements are seen only on activation, but not all investigators follow that convention. The total AIMS score is calculated as the sum of items 1-7; dental items are not included in this total. This implementation subtracts 1 point for movements seen only on activation before summing the total score

[111804-1] Positive Childhood Experiences [Positive Childhood Experiences] -- **Component:** Positive Childhood Experiences **Property:** - **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** PCEs  **Definition:** The Positive Childhood Experiences (PCEs) survey instrument is a brief, validated tool designed to assess the presence of supportive and protective factors in childhood that promote resilience and long-term well-being. [112280-3] The Home Energy Insecurity Scale [HEIS] -- **Component:** The Home Energy Insecurity Scale **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 12Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** HEIS  **Definition:** The Home Energy Insecurity Scale represents a household level scale that is consistent with other Results Oriented Management and Accountability (ROMA)-based household level outcome scales. The basic structure of a ROMA scale (thriving, capable, stable, vulnerable, in-crisis) can be directly applied to home energy situations to measure the self-sufficiency status of a household.

[112301-7] Leukocyte deformability sepsis assessment [Interpretation] in Blood by Microfluidic deformability cytometer -- **Component:** Leukocyte deformability sepsis assessment **Property:** Imp **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Bld **Scale:** OrdQn **Method:** Cytometry.microfluidic  **Short name:** Lukcyt deform sepsis assessmnt Bld Microfluid deform cytmtr-Imp  **Definition:** This term represents a test which measures biophysical and morphological changes in leukocytes (white blood cells) that occur during immune activation and dysregulation, with a specific focus on detecting sepsis. The test analyzes circulating neutrophils, monocytes, and other white blood cells using microfluidic technology to quantify cellular mechanical properties-particularly visco-elastic inertial response (VEIR): which reflects cell density, size, deformability, and viscosity. These biophysical properties change dramatically during leukocyte activation, including nuclear rearrangement and the early stages of neutrophil extracellular trap (NET) formation, processes characteristic of dysregulated immunity in sepsis. [PMID:37189536]

[112480-9] DePaul Questionnaire - Post-Exertional Malaise (DSQ-PEM) [DePaul Questionnaire - Post-Exertional Malaise] -- **Component:** DePaul Questionnaire - Post-Exertional Malaise (DSQ-PEM) **Property:** - **Time aspect:** 6Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** - **Method:** DSQ-PEM  **Definition:** The DePaul Symptom Questionnaire - Post-Exertional Malaise (DSQ-PEM) is a validated screening questionnaire designed to assess the frequency and severity of symptoms associated with post-exertional malaise (PEM). It evaluates exertion-induced worsening of symptoms such as fatigue, pain, cognitive impairments, and autonomic dysfunction. The questionnaire helps objectively diagnose PEM and distinguish it from other forms of fatigue. It is used in clinical practice and research to determine individual limits of exertion.
[112481-7] Total score [DePaul Questionnaire - Post-Exertional Malaise] -- **Component:** Total score **Property:** Score **Time aspect:** 6Mo **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Qn **Method:** DSQ-PEM  **Definition:** DSQ-PEM Scoring   Scoring Step 1 Items 1-5: A frequency and severity score of 2, 2 on any items 1-5 is indicative of PEM.  Scoring Step 2 Items 7, 8: Either item 7 or 8 must have an answer of yes to indicate an ME and/or CFS dx. Item 9: A response of >14 h is needed to indicate an ME and/or CFS dx. Items 6, 10: Neither item indicates an ME and/or CFS diagnosis, but provides a description of patient PEM for clinical evaluations.

[11329-0] History general Narrative - Reported -- **Component:** History general **Property:** Find **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Patient **Scale:** Nar **Method:** Reported  **Short name:** Hx general Reported  **Definition:** History general describes all aspects of medical history of the patient even if not pertinent to the current procedure, and may include chief complaint, past medical history, social history, family history, surgical or procedure history, medication history, and other history information. It may also be reported as a collection of random clinical statements or it may be reported categorically.  Categorical report formats may be divided into multiple subsections, including Past Medical History and Social History.

[11628-5] Fetal Head Binocular distance estimated from Biparietal diameter on US -- **Component:** Binocular distance **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Head^Fetus **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US+Estimated from BPD  **Short name:** Fet Head Binoc D from BPD  **Definition:** Predicted from biparietal diameter measurement
[11629-3] Fetal Head Binocular distance US -- **Component:** Binocular distance **Property:** Len **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** Head^Fetus **Scale:** Qn **Method:** US.measured  **Short name:** Fet Head Binoc D US.meas  **Definition:** Measured from the lateral edge of one orbit to the lateral edge of the other in a coronal plane in which both orbits are symmetrical and each of the largest possible diameter.
[11630-1] Fetal Biophysical profile.amniotic fluid volume US -- **Component:** Biophysical profile.amniotic fluid volume **Property:** Fcn **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Fetus **Scale:** Ord **Method:** US  **Short name:** Fet BPP.amn fld vol US  **Definition:** At least one pocket of amniotic fluid measuring 2 cm in two perpendicular planes
[11631-9] Fetal Biophysical profile.body movement US -- **Component:** Biophysical profile.body movement **Property:** Fcn **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Fetus **Scale:** Ord **Method:** US  **Short name:** Fet BPP.body movement US  **Definition:** At least three discrete body/limb movements in 30 minutes
[11632-7] Fetal Biophysical profile.breathing movement US -- **Component:** Biophysical profile.breathing movement **Property:** Fcn **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Fetus **Scale:** Ord **Method:** US  **Short name:** Fet BPP.breathing movement US  **Definition:** At least one episode of >30 seconds duration in 30 minutes of observation
[11633-5] Fetal Heart rate reactivity US -- **Component:** Heart rate reactivity **Property:** Fcn **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Fetus **Scale:** Ord **Method:** US  **Short name:** Fetal NST by US  **Definition:** Heart rates are considered reactive ("normal") if two or more accelerations occur within a 20-minute period. Nonreactive heart rate  is one in which not enough accelerations are detected in a 40-minute period and can be associated with several findings, including the fetus was asleep during the test in which case the test time may be extended. Nonreactive results can also occur if the mother is taking certain medications or that the fetus is not getting enough oxygen.[https://www.acog.org/]
[11634-3] Fetal Biophysical profile.sum Derived -- **Component:** Biophysical profile.sum **Property:** Fcn **Time aspect:** Pt **System:** ^Fetus **Scale:** Ord **Method:** Derived  **Short name:** Fet BPP.sum Derived  **Definition:** Sum of fetal heart rate reactivity, fetal body movement, fetal tone, fetal breathing movement and amniotic fluid volume scores of 0 or 2 each. Result can be 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, or 10
